review_comment: After looking how `tests/net/socket/tcp` is doing things when connecting, I think you need to let other threads to run too, so `k_msleep()` might help here.
Good call. I was occasionally unable to connect and thought it may have been scheduler related, although @rlubos hinted that may have been due to not having enough buffers / packets.
Definitely seeing things hanging on `mps2_an385`, but adding a `k_msleep()` after connect does not seem to help.

You need to set the hostname, otherwise mbedTLS will reject the server certificate. The certificate from echo samples was issued for the `localhost` so the following will do the job:
```
setsockopt(client_fd, SOL_TLS, TLS_HOSTNAME, "localhost", sizeof("localhost"));
```
Hmm... if `CONFIG_NET_HOSTNAME_ENABLE` is not set, should a TLS example always fall back to "localhost" then? That could be one of the issues I was having with Greybus.

It has nothing to do with `CONFIG_NET_HOSTNAME_ENABLE` - `TLS_HOSTNAME` option specifies the name of the host for which the certificate was issued for (which is part of the certificate itslef, so called "Common Name"). When reaching some public server, it should match the hostname you connect to (see https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/master/samples/net/sockets/http_get/src/http_get.c#L105 for example).

Here, the certificates used by echo were created with Common Name set to `localhost`, so we need to use this name here.

It's also possible to skip hostname validation by explicitly setting this option to a NULL string, but it's isn't considered as safe practice.
K - not sure if all of the issues are fixed, but I added the `TLS_HOSTNAME`

```
E: Data buffer (133) allocation failed.

```

The first test passes, but it would appear that there is a memory leak somewhere, as the second and third tests are starved for memory.

Running my tests on `qemu_x86_64`, but the stack sizes needed here are enormous 8192 bytes for the client and server. I guess on `qemu_cortex_m3` that would probably only be 4096, but still.. a lot of resources.
Doesn't seem to be enough ram to run on `qemu_cortex_m3`

> Running my tests on `qemu_x86_64`, but the stack sizes needed here are enormous 8192 bytes for the client and server. I guess on `qemu_cortex_m3` that would probably only be 4096, but still.. a lot of resources.

Yes, mbedtls is a memory hog that requires lot of memory to function properly. Also you do not get nice information when running out of memory, things just fail in weird ways.

You can enable `CONFIG_STACK_SENTINEL` to detect stack overflows. The other issue looks like there's too little net bufs.

btw. You may expect delayed answers from my side, I started my christmas break today 🎄
